name: ADIAL PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.
business: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on the development of therapeutics for the treatment or prevention of addiction and related disorders. Our lead investigational new drug candidate, AD04, is being developed as a therapeutic agent for the treatment of alcohol use disorder (AUD) in patients with specific genetic biomarkers identified using a companion diagnostic genetic test. We currently do not have any products approved for sale and have not generated any significant revenue since our inception.
sellsTo: patients with alcohol use disorder (AUD) who have certain target genotypes
addiction specialists and primary care physicians treating AUD

exposures.sentence: Our investigational new drug candidate, AD04, is being developed as a therapeutic agent for the treatment of alcohol use disorder (AUD).

AD04 was investigated in a Phase 3 clinical trial, designated the ONWARD trial, for the potential treatment of AUD in subjects with certain target genotypes, which were identified using our companion diagnostic genetic test.

catalysts.event: Collaboration framework agreement with Molteni Farmaceutici for commercialization of AD04 in Europe
catalysts.sentence: On March 3, 2026, we entered into a collaboration framework agreement with a strategic partner, Molteni Farmaceutici (Molteni), for a proposed exclusive partnership covering the commercialization of AD04 in Europe.

catalysts.event: Plan to begin a Phase 3 study of AD04 in 2026
catalysts.sentence: In addition, we plan to begin a Phase 3 study of AD04 in 2026, pending availability of adequate funds, to complete production of sufficient drug product to carry out the study, and to begin the process of clinical validation of our new cheek swab diagnostic genetic test, which will be conducted with the Phase 3 study.

tamClaims.claim: 27.9 million people age 12 and older had AUD in the United States alone according to the 2024 National Survey on Drug Use and Health.
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States alone, the 2024 National Survey on Drug Use and Health (NSDUH) reported approximately 27.9 million people age 12 and older had AUD.

tamClaims.claim: Among those with AUD, 21.4% (5.97 million) were classified as moderate and 19.2% (5.35 million) as severe.
tamClaims.sentence: Among those reporting AUD, 21.4% or 5.97M were classified as moderate and 19.2% or 5.35M were classified as Severe.

tamClaims.claim: Only 2.5% (697,000) of people with AUD received medication for treatment.
tamClaims.sentence: Among the 27.9 million, only 2.5% or 697,000 people received medication for AUD treatment.
